title: Create rectangles with a given area
description: Practice finding the area of rectangles by counting unit squares. Create rectangles with a given area by covering unit squares.
topic_path: Finding area by decomposition and rearranging
Create rectangles with a given area

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]] is $1$ unit square.   

**Which rectangles have an area of $8$ square units?**  

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The area is the amount of flat space a shape takes up. 

We can find the area by counting the number of unit squares that the shape covers.
These rectangles do not have an area of $8$ square units.  



[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ image 3]]

These rectangles have an area of $8$ square units.



[[☃ image 1]]



[[☃ image 2]]

choices.content: ![A rectangle with 1 row has 6 unit squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/91efe3ac03ce282443d3c1033ba850bcf9aa4a9c)
![A rectangle with 2 rows. Each row has 5 unit squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1cb04c8b79f627c9af3d215062aaa29c37d6e2c5)
![A rectangle with 3 rows. Each row has 3 unit squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/aa06d89bef394b8146da0ea1a7f75c3c22983cab)
![A rectangle with 1 row has 8 unit squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5e5ba01ad61f317bdd35ad5f9485894327479429)
![A rectangle with 4 rows. Each row has 2 unit squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bf26a80c8dd9568f5994e2d82e06891ee3575a39)

assessment_items.question_content:
Jalen drew $2$ of the same rectangle with an area of $6$ square units. She turned one sideways.

**Move the corners of the other figure to make a *different* rectangle with the *same area*.**  
*NO sides should be $3$ units long.*

[[☃ interactive-graph 1]]
hints:
The area is the amount of flat space a shape takes up. 

We can find the area by counting the number of unit squares that the shape covers.

The area of Jalen's rectangle is $6$ square units.

[[☃ image 1]]

Shapes can look different but still have the same area. 

Let’s find more shapes that can have an area of $6$ square units but have different side lengths than Jalen's rectangle.

One way to make a rectangle with an area of $6$ square units is with $1$ row of $6$ square units anywhere on the graph.

[[☃ image 1]]

Another way to make a rectangle with an area of $6$ square units is with $6$ rows of $1$ square unit anywhere on the graph.

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ interactive-graph 1]]
hints:

Let’s find more shapes that can have an area of $8$ square units but have different side lengths than Keone's rectangle.
One way to make a rectangle with an area of $8$ square units is with $2$ rows of $4$ square units anywhere on the graph.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each small square on the grid has an area of $1$ square unit.

**Move the corners of the figure around to make a square with an area of $9$ square units.**
[[☃ interactive-graph 1]]
hints:
The area is the amount of flat space a shape takes up. 

Our square must cover an area with $9$ square units inside.

We can make a square with an area of $9$ square units with $3$ rows of $3$ squares.  This square could be anywhere on the graph.


[[☃ image 1]]
